paper_title: STUDY ON THE NICKEL ADSORPTION CHARACTERISTICS OF LAKE SEDIMENTS AND SOIL

methods.description: This method involves using the Freundlich and Elovich equations to analyze the adsorption capacity and strength of samples to nickel (Ni). It helps in understanding how well the samples can adsorb Ni and the dynamics of the adsorption process.

steps.description: Use the Freundlich equation to calculate lgKf and 1/n, representing the adsorption capacity and adsorption strength of the sample to Ni.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate adsorption capacity and strength using Freundlich equation

steps.description: Use the Elovich equation to determine the maximum adsorption capacity (qm), adsorption energy constant (K), and adsorption potential (ε).
steps.searchable_summary: determine adsorption parameters using Elovich equation

methods.description: This method uses the parabolic diffusion equation to analyze the adsorption rate and correlation between variables. It helps in understanding the rate at which adsorption occurs and the relationship between different variables.

steps.description: Apply the parabolic diffusion equation to calculate equilibrium adsorption capacity (CNi) and adsorption capacity at time t (C).
steps.searchable_summary: calculate equilibrium and time-based adsorption capacities using parabolic diffusion equation

steps.description: Calculate the correlation coefficient (R) and test value (P) to assess the correlation between variables.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate correlation coefficient and test value
